content.subtitle: Uses
items: When we don’t know who does/did something
When it’s obvious who does/did something
When it’s not important who does/did something

content.subtitle: Examples
items: The car was stolen at approximately 1.30 am.
Having been introduced in 1988, the Road Traffic Act regulates all vehicle use on UK roads.
The XL500 was designed with young families in mind, so there’s plenty of room in the boot.
This type of submarine was developed during the Second World War by the Americans.
We were surprised by the number of people trying to leave the city for the long weekend.
All passengers are required to present their ticket to the inspector.

content.subtitle: Form
items: noun + verb in passive form + infinitive / perfect infinitive
There + verb in passive form + infinitive / perfect infinitive
It + verb in passive form + that clause

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Tourism is expected to become a major part of the country’s economy.
There are reported to have been a record number of accidents on the roads this year.
It is thought that the new railway will provide employment opportunities for local people.

content.subtitle: Form
items: Some verbs (bring, buy, get, give, leave, lend, make, offer, owe, pass, pay, promise, refuse, send, show, take, teach, tell, write) can take two objects.

content.subtitle: Example
items: Active: Michael gave the plane tickets to Jill.
Passive (indirect object as subject): Jill was given the plane tickets (by Michael).
Passive (direct object as subject): The plane tickets were given to Jill (by Michael).

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: With explain and suggest, the preposition cannot be omitted. They cannot be used with indirect object as subject.

content.subtitle: Tenses to avoid
content.description: The passive is not normally used with present perfect continuous, past perfect continuous, future continuous or future perfect continuous.

content.subtitle: Examples
items: ✓ Preparations for the flight will be in progress as the President arrives.
✓ At the end of this year, I will have been in training as a pilot for four years.
✓ Vintage cars have been on display in the town centre all this week.
✓ By the time they came to a decision, the problem had been under consideration for some time.
✓ The new railway station has been under construction for two years now.

content.subtitle: Uses
items: Actions we arrange for other people to do for us
Things we experience (usually negative and not intended)

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Did you finally get your bike fixed?
I heard that Susie had her motorbike stolen.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: Get is more informal than have.
Can use need, want, would like: I’d like those cars washed by this evening.
Get sth done can also mean 'finish doing something': We’ll set off as soon as I’ve got the car fixed.

sections.title: Causative: get sb to do / have sb do

content.subtitle: Form
items: Actions we make somebody/something do for us

content.subtitle: Example
items: Did you get Alex to drive you all the way to London?

content.subtitle: Form
items: Actions we make somebody/something start doing

content.subtitle: Example
items: Don’t worry. We’ll soon have your car running like new.

sections.title: A: Correct the Passive Form
sections.instructions: If the verb in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, rewrite it correctly.

items.sentence: Could you tell me where your passport **was been issued**?
When the winning actor **was finally announced**, a big cheer went up.
**Has** your business trip to Calcutta next week **confirmed** by the Indian office?
Everyone who **was been invited** to the wedding came.
The wire coat hanger **invented** in 1903 by a Mr Parkhouse.
The issue **has discussed** in Parliament yesterday, but no decisions were taken.
By the time we get there, the problem **should have been sorted out**.
Britain **was be invaded** by the Romans under Emperor Claudius in 43 AD.
The party **had been planned** months in advance, but we still had problems.
After **be treated**, I was allowed to leave the hospital.
We boarded the ship and **were showing** to our cabin by a steward.
The new supermarket **is to be opened** by an actor from a soap opera.

sections.title: B: Passive Verb Forms
sections.instructions: Write a verb from the box in the correct passive form in each gap.

items.sentence: The stolen items ……………………. in the attic of an empty house last week.
When the open-air cinema opens, the first film ……………………… will be Vertigo.
Questions …………………………… in Parliament about the Minister’s business interests.
We’re looking forward to ………………………… a guided tour of the whole factory.
Just imagine that when these words ……………………… , Europeans had not yet discovered America.
Once the barbecue ………………………… , we’ll be able to start cooking.
Before ……………………………… to see the invention, I had to agree not to tell anyone.
I would really like ………………………… at the airport, if that’s possible.
It ………………………………… very clear to us that we weren’t welcome.
Gareth says he’s always wanted …………………………… as someone with a good sense of humour.

sections.title: C: Impersonal Passive
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Global warming is thought **to be caused / is caused** by pollution.
There **are to be estimated / are estimated** to be about 5,400 different species of mammals.
It **is to be reported / has been reported** that gunshots have been heard in the city centre.
The escaped prisoner is believed **to use / to have used** a rope to climb the wall.
It **is to be calculated / is calculated** that about 60% of a man’s body mass is water.
Over 200 people **are being understood / are understood** to have been injured in the explosion.
It is considered impolite in some cultures **to be eaten / to eat** with your left hand.
There **mean / were meant** to have been 60 crates of olives in the order, not 600!
**It / There** has been suggested that people who don’t recycle should pay more tax.
Your order is guaranteed **will be delivered / to be delivered** within three working days.

sections.title: D: Error Correction (Heathrow Text)
sections.instructions: Each sentence contains one verb in the wrong form. Underline the incorrect word or phrase and rewrite it correctly.

items.sentence: Heathrow Airport, near London, **has called** the world’s busiest international airport...
The land on which the airport stands **sold** to Fairey Aviation...
...was developed for civilian use, **was named** after a small village...
...the first terminal building, which **has later renamed** Terminal 2...
...meaning that passengers could **be transported** from central London...
Four passenger terminals... **have been constructed** and permission was granted...
...a new airport... **has been built** on a floating island...

sections.title: E: Avoiding the Passive
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences describing each situation using the phrase given.

items.sentence: By the time I take my exams, ......
items.answer: I will have been in training as an accountant for one year.

items.sentence: The redevelopment of the city centre ......
items.answer: has been under consideration for some time.

items.sentence: Discussions on the issue ......
items.answer: have been in progress for over two weeks.

items.sentence: The local Art Society’s latest works ......
items.answer: will be on display at the library this weekend.

items.sentence: George’s website ......
items.answer: seems to have been under construction for a long time.

sections.title: F: Causative Error Correction
sections.instructions: If the word or phrase in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, rewrite it correctly.

items.sentence: We had to have it repaired twice! (1)
Well, we got delivered it to our house on Monday morning. (2)
We decided to have it check straight away... (3)
Oh, I didn’t know you’d had them do that. (4)
We had my dad to have a look at it. (5)
items.answer: had my dad have a look

items.sentence: Did you have the company to come to fix it? (6)
We got my brother to working on it. (7)
I bet he soon had running it smoothly. (8)
...we still had to get the company come out in the end... (9)
You should get them to give you some compensation. (10)

sections.title: G: Causative Structures
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence using the noun in bold and an appropriate causative form of the verb in bold.

items.sentence: If you don’t understand, ...... it to you again after the lesson.
...watching my kitten play with a ball of yarn soon ...... much better.
Don’t bother redecorating yourself – ...... it.
...... was so annoying because it meant I had to take the tube to work.
You won’t learn as much if you ...... you with your homework all the time.
...the band soon ...... to their hit songs.
Don’t try ...... your dirty work for you!
Have you thought about ...... as short as mine?
I ...... me how to juggle with five balls, but I still couldn’t do it.
Have you heard about the problems caused by students ...... by companies on the Internet?

sections.title: H: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Use between three and eight words.

items.sentence: They made me wait for over 20 minutes on the phone!
Although people consider Ashley to be a star, she is always friendly...
We have had reports that the Prime Minister is making a surprise visit to Syria.
The manager provided the staff with extra training.
items.answer: staff were provided with extra training by

items.sentence: I think someone needs to explain this computer program to me.
items.answer: someone to explain this computer program to

items.sentence: They have been building the new stadium for much longer...
Do you know why they made Craig stay behind after the lesson?
Call Lionel and ask him to send the reports up to my office.
When Nadine arrived, she soon ...... at all her jokes.
The government must ...... strong measures against crime.
items.answer: D be seen to be taking

items.sentence: Mrs Davies asked me to tell you that she would like ...... by Friday at the latest.
Joining a gym ...... , but I didn’t really fancy the idea.
There ...... between 4,000 and 6,000 languages in the world...
...he says he ...... while he was on holiday.
Why didn’t you ...... you when you moved house?
Profits ...... slightly next year...
It’s hard to believe that this film ...... completely by computer.
As soon as Dad ...... , we can leave.
items.answer: B has got the car started

sections.title: J: Open Cloze (The Scramjet)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Imagine (1) ...... transported around the globe...
...rather than the 24 hours which (2) ...... currently required...
...a highly experimental type of engine which has been (3) ...... development...
In a conventional jet engine, air (4) ...... sucked into the engine...
...where it is mixed (5) ...... fuel, ignited...
In a scramjet, which has to (6) ...... taken to supersonic speeds...
It has (7) ...... estimated that a scramjet might be capable...
However, the technology (8) ...... been found to be extremely difficult to control.
Although scientists (9) ...... had scramjets perform short flights...
...and indeed may never (10) ...... successfully developed.
